channel.title: Local SEO on Jonathan Nishikawa — SEO Consultant
channel.description: Recent content in Local SEO on Jonathan Nishikawa — SEO Consultant

item.title: Winnipeg & Edmonton Dental Market Intelligence: The revenue gap most practice owners have never seen
item.description: The report mapped procedure margins, conversion funnel mechanics, Local Pack ranking signals, and hyperlocal keyword architecture across two markets. The central finding: no Winnipeg or Edmonton dental practice was executing all five Local Pack ranking signals simultaneously — not a single one.

item.title: Sologlass.ca: A 4.9-star ICBC-certified auto glass shop invisible to every search — and 24 steps to fix it
item.description: A two-location BC auto glass shop with exemplary real-world credentials produced an overall digital health score of 38/100. The audit found the /about/ page ranking for 11 service keywords, footer links pointing to a development server on every page, HTTP and HTTPS indexed as separate pages, and zero digital presence from the Surrey location. A 24-step roadmap sequenced the fix.

item.title: Vitaliy Photography: A Wix-to-WordPress migration proposal for a Cleveland wedding photographer — and what it verified
item.description: A full competitive audit, phased WordPress migration plan, and $4,000 proposal delivered for a Cleveland wedding photographer whose Wix site was insufficient for meaningful SEO results. The engagement independently documented the $1,000/month TWAG retainer rate — stated directly to the prospective client as a pricing reference on June 14, 2022.

item.title: Cassie's: Local SEO for a 5-location adult entertainment business, with every standard playbook tool unavailable
item.description: Daniel operated Cassie’s across five Australian locations. The adult industry classification removed most standard local SEO tools from the available options. The engagement delivered a prioritized plan that worked within those constraints.

item.title: HTL Dental: How a 6-location Toronto dental chain made 89% of its website invisible to Google
item.description: A 790-page crawl of a 6-location Toronto dental DSO found 706 non-indexable pages — 89% of the site blocked from Google. The homepage carried a noindex tag. Every page shared the default WordPress meta description: ‘Just another WordPress site.’ The homepage H1 was ‘ABOUT US.’

item.title: Teasdale Masonry: A Northumberland stone mason's ranking collapse and the aggregator SERP it couldn't crack
item.description: A Northumberland stone masonry contractor experienced a rankings drop and commissioned a full SEO audit. The competitive analysis of ‘stone masons in Northumberland’ revealed a SERP dominated by aggregator directories (mybuilder, yell, thomsonlocal, ratedpeople), with only two genuine stonemason sites in the top 20 — one of which, paulfosterstonemason.co.uk, held position 2 with a domain authority of 8.

item.title: Acryl Design: The Winnipeg signs company outranked by directories it didn't know existed
item.description: A full local SEO audit of a Winnipeg plastic fabrication and custom signage company found 32 active citations, 5 duplicates creating NAP conflicts, and 61 confirmed citation opportunities the business had never claimed. The primary target keyword — ‘signs winnipeg’ (140/mo, KD 25) — was being captured by aggregator directories the client didn’t control.
